creator: destination_wellknown
caption: The only reason why you should pay extra👇  Right, so the Uber Boats by Thames Clippers are basically like a water bus that the local Londoners use to get into the city centre from areas outside of Zone 1 🤓  So they sail pretty fast, and there’s no information for tourists who might want to hear a bit about the places you sail past.   If you enjoy that kind of commentary, the (pricier) tourist boats are definitely the best. Especially if it doesn’t rain, as you can sit outside and on top of the deck.   But if you just want to experience London from the river Thames or you want to get out and experience an area like Greenwich (which I highly recommend), then the Uber Boats are both faster and cheaper 🙌  What would you prefer - the pricier touristy boats with commentary or the faster & cheaper Uber Boat? ✌️  Btw, send me a DM saying “London” or comment “ebook” here, and I’ll send you the link to my FREE London e-book where I share all my top London travel advice 🤝  #londontravel #londontrip #traveltips #thamesriver

transcription.text: I hate gatekeeping, but I love travel tips, so here's one if you're ever in London. You don't have to pay £13 to go on a river cruise. Just get the Uber boat, which will cost you from £5.60 if you pay contactless or with an Oyster card. The route is obviously the same, and on these boats there's even a bar where you can get a drink, or a Snickers if you're a bit cranky.
